title: Women’s Impact Award 2026: Global Call for Women Researchers Driving Scientific Excellence and Societal Impact
summary: The Women’s Impact Award 2026, presented by the Falling Walls Foundation in collaboration with the Elsevier Foundation, is a prestigious global recognition designed to celebrate outstanding women researchers whose work combines scientific excellence with measurable societal impact. As part of the broader Global Call for Science Breakthroughs 2026, this award highlights transformative research that addresses pressing global challenges while promoting equity, inclusion, and sustainable development. It is an opportunity for leading women scientists and researchers worldwide to gain international recognition, expand their influence, and contribute to shaping a more equitable future. About the Women’s Impact Award The Women’s Impact Award is a flagship initiative that honors women who are not only advancing scientific knowledge but also applying their research to create tangible, real-world change. The award specifically recognizes: Scientific excellence at the highest level Research with clear societal relevance and impact Contributions that advance equity, inclusion, and justice Leadership in addressing global and community-level challenges This initiative is part of the Falling Walls Foundation’s mission to identify and celebrate breakthroughs in science that have the potential to change the world. About the Global Call for Science Breakthroughs 2026 The Global Call for Science Breakthroughs is an international platform that invites nominations and applications from leading researchers across disciplines. It provides a stage for groundbreaking work that pushes the boundaries of knowledge and contributes to solving critical global issues. The Women’s Impact Award is a dedicated category within this global call, focusing specifically on women and gender-diverse researchers. What the Award Recognizes The Women’s Impact Award celebrates research projects that combine academic rigor with societal relevance. Applicants must demonstrate that their work contributes meaningfully to one or more of the following areas: 1. Advancing Gender Equality through Research Eligible projects may include: Research addressing gender bias in fields such as artificial intelligence, healthcare, education, finance, or employment Studies that incorporate sex and gender differences in data collection, analysis, and interpretation Projects using gender-sensitive methodologies, including: Gender-disaggregated data Co-design approaches involving women and gender-diverse populations Interdisciplinary research that promotes inclusive innovation 2. Supporting Underserved and Marginalized Communities Applicants may also qualify if their research focuses on empowering underserved populations through: Participatory research involving communities such as: Refugees Rural populations People with disabilities Low-income groups Development of technologies, policies, or interventions that improve: Access to healthcare Educational opportunities Justice and legal systems Safety and protection Economic inclusion Research outcomes that actively reduce inequality or dismantle systemic barriers Projects are not required to focus exclusively on women or girls, but they must clearly demonstrate a commitment to equity, inclusion, and positive societal transformation. Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the Women’s Impact Award 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: 1. Career Level Applicants must be at: Postdoctoral level or above, or An equivalent level of professional independence and responsibility Eligible roles include: Postdoctoral researchers Research fellows Senior researchers Principal investigators Group leaders Professors 2. Inclusive Eligibility The award embraces diversity and inclusivity. Applications are welcome from: Cisgender women Transgender women Genderqueer individuals Non-binary individuals This inclusive approach ensures representation across diverse gender identities within the scientific community. Research Project Requirements Applicants must be actively engaged in a research project at the time of submission. Eligible Project Stages: Data collection or analysis Pilot or testing phases Implementation in real-world settings Ineligible Projects: Projects at the idea stage Concepts under development Proposals seeking funding without active implementation Language Requirement: All applications must be submitted in English Who Can Nominate Nominations for the Women’s Impact Award 2026 can be submitted by a wide range of institutions and individuals, including: Universities and higher education institutions Research organizations and institutes Companies with research laboratories Scientific foundations Partner organizations of the Falling Walls Foundation Leading scientists and experts in relevant fields Applicants may also apply directly, depending on the nomination pathway. Benefits of the Women’s Impact Award While specific prize details may vary, recipients of the Women’s Impact Award typically benefit from: Global recognition at a prestigious international platform Visibility among leading scientists, policymakers, and innovators Opportunities to expand professional networks Increased credibility and influence in their field Access to future collaborations and funding opportunities Notable Past Winners The Women’s Impact Award has previously recognized exceptional researchers whose work has made significant contributions to science and society. Some recent awardees include: Ângela Gonçalves (German Cancer Research Center) Colette Wabnitz (Stanford Center for Ocean Solutions; University of British Columbia) Omneya Attallah (Arab Academy for Science, Technology, and Maritime Transport) These winners exemplify the caliber of research and impact the award seeks to celebrate. Application Process Interested candidates are encouraged to apply or be nominated through the Official Global Call for Science Breakthroughs platform. Steps to Apply: Prepare detailed information about your research project Demonstrate scientific excellence and societal impact Submit your application in English Ensure your project meets all eligibility criteria Application Deadline: 15-Apr-2026 Why You Should Apply The Women’s Impact Award 2026 offers a unique opportunity to: Showcase your research on a global stage Gain recognition for impactful scientific work Contribute to advancing gender equality and inclusion Join a network of leading researchers and innovators Strengthen your academic and professional profile Learn more about the Women’s Impact Award 2026 HERE. Click HERE to discover more global Award opportunities on OFY. The post Women’s Impact Award 2026: Global Call for Women Researchers Driving Scientific Excellence and Societal Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town
summary: If you are a young leader passionate about creating real change in your community, the Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 offers one of the most prestigious fully funded opportunities to gain global exposure, leadership training, and international recognition. This scholarship, offered by One Young World in partnership with the Capital City of Podgorica, provides a unique pathway to attend the globally acclaimed One Young World Summit 2026 . About the Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship is a highly competitive, fully funded opportunity designed to support one exceptional young leader from Podgorica to participate in a global leadership experience. Podgorica, one of Europe’s youngest capitals and a future European Youth Capital 2028, is actively investing in youth empowerment, innovation, and civic participation. Through this initiative, selected scholars will join thousands of global changemakers at the One Young World Summit, where leaders from over 190 countries gather to address pressing global challenges such as: Climate change and environmental protection Social inequality and education Youth participation and governance Entrepreneurship and innovation What Makes the One Young World Summit So Valuable? The One Young World Summit 2026 is one of the most influential global gatherings of young leaders. It brings together: Entrepreneurs and innovators Activists and policy advocates Students and professionals Social impact leaders Participants gain access to world-class mentorship, networking opportunities, and leadership development programs that can transform their careers and amplify their impact globally. Scholarship Benefits (Fully Funded Package) The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 provides comprehensive financial and professional support, including: 1. Full Access to the Global Summit Participation in all sessions of the One Young World Summit 2026 2. Travel Coverage Round-trip international flights (economy class) 3. Accommodation Hotel stay from 2 November to 7 November 2026 4. Meals and Catering Breakfast at the hotel Lunch and dinner during the Summit 5. Local Transportation Transport between accommodation and Summit venue 6. Visa Support Visa application costs and related travel expenses covered 7. Leadership Development Opportunities Access to the One Young World Global Leadership Programme Participation in the Action Accelerator Lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador Community Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates) This opportunity is highly specific and targeted. Applicants must meet all the following requirements: Geographic Eligibility Must be a national of Montenegro Must be living and actively contributing in Podgorica Age Requirement Must be between 18 and 30 years old by November 2026 Language Requirement Must have a strong working knowledge of English Availability Must be available full-time from 2–7 November 2026 Target Audience (Who This Opportunity Is For) This scholarship is ideal for: Students Young professionals Social entrepreneurs Activists and advocates Innovators and community leaders Applicants must demonstrate a strong commitment to creating impact in areas such as: Environmental sustainability Youth engagement and participation Cultural development Digital innovation Education and equality Entrepreneurship Selection Criteria (How Winners Are Chosen) The selection process is rigorous and based on multiple impact-driven factors: 1. Proven Impact Applicants must show measurable contributions to positive change in Podgorica or Montenegro. 2. Community Engagement Evidence of active participation in: Volunteering Civic initiatives Youth programs 3. Academic or Professional Excellence Strong achievements in education, career, or extracurricular activities. 4. Vision for Future Impact A clear plan for how the Summit experience will be used to benefit the local community. 5. Leadership Potential Ability to represent Podgorica on a global stage and act as a youth ambassador. 6. Alignment with Global Values Commitment to building a fair, sustainable, and inclusive future. Important Dates Applications Opened: 29 January 2026 Summit Dates: 3–6 November 2026 Missing the deadline automatically disqualifies your application, so early submission is strongly recommended. Why You Should Apply This is more than just a scholarship—it is a career-defining opportunity. Scholars gain: Global recognition and credibility Access to an elite international network Exposure to world leaders and changemakers Opportunities for future funding and collaborations Long-term leadership development According to program insights, participants return with stronger networks, enhanced skills, and greater capacity to drive impact locally and globally. Apply here Deadline 23rd March 2026 The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 stands out as a highly prestigious, fully funded opportunity for young leaders committed to meaningful change. However, it is geographically restricted to Montenegro, making it extremely competitive within that pool. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026: €60,000 Award for Global Leaders Advancing Gender Equality
summary: The Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026 is a prestigious international award presented by the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe (PACE), in partnership with the Government of Iceland. This highly respected prize recognizes and rewards outstanding individuals, organizations, and initiatives that have demonstrated exceptional commitment to advancing women’s empowerment and gender equality across the world. Named after Vigdís Finnbogadóttir, the first woman in history to be democratically elected as a Head of State, this award celebrates transformative leadership, inclusive policies, and impactful actions that promote the full and equal participation of women and girls in all spheres of life. With a monetary award of 60,000 euros and global recognition, the Vigdís Prize stands as one of the most significant honors dedicated to gender equality and women’s rights. About the Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment Launched in 2023, the Vigdís Prize aims to spotlight initiatives and leaders who are making meaningful contributions toward empowering women and girls in diverse contexts. The award specifically recognizes efforts that: Advance gender equality at local, national, or global levels Promote equal participation in political, social, and economic decision-making Foster inclusive policies and practices Address systemic barriers affecting women and girls Support diversity and inclusion across all communities At a time when gender equality continues to face challenges globally, this prize serves as a powerful platform to highlight impactful work and inspire further progress. Prize Value and Recognition The Vigdís Prize offers both financial and symbolic recognition to its winner: A monetary award of 60,000 euros A prestigious trophy International recognition and visibility Opportunity to present work on a global stage The award ceremony takes place in Strasbourg, France, during the Summer Session of the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe. Key Dates for 2026 Applicants and nominators should take note of the following timeline: 21 November 2025: Call for candidates opened 27 March 2026: Deadline for submission of nominations May 2026: First meeting of the Selection Panel in Reykjavik 21 May 2026: Shortlisting of three candidates 21 June 2026: Final selection of the winner in Strasbourg 22 June 2026: Official award ceremony Who Can Be Nominated The Vigdís Prize is open to a wide range of nominees who are actively contributing to women’s empowerment. Eligible nominees include: Individuals Civil society organizations Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Associations Private companies Both national and international candidates are eligible, reflecting the global scope of the award. Areas of Impact Recognized Nominees should demonstrate significant achievements in one or more of the following areas: 1. Advancing Gender Equality Promoting equal rights and opportunities for women and girls Addressing discrimination and gender-based inequalities Supporting inclusive governance and leadership 2. Empowering Women and Girls Initiatives that enhance access to education, healthcare, and economic opportunities Programs that support women’s leadership and participation Actions that protect women’s rights and dignity 3. Promoting Inclusive Participation Encouraging women’s involvement in decision-making processes Creating platforms for marginalized voices Strengthening representation across sectors Nomination Requirements To successfully submit a nomination for the Vigdís Prize 2026, the following requirements must be met: 1. Nomination Form Complete the Official Nomination Form Provide detailed information about the nominee and their work 2. Language Applications must be submitted in either English or French 3. Sponsorship Requirement Each nomination must be supported by at least five sponsors Sponsors must provide their signatures using the official sponsors’ form 4. Submission Format All documents must be compiled into a single PDF file File naming format: For organizations: NAME – Country For individuals: FAMILY NAME – First Name – Country 5. Submission Process Upload the complete application package through the official submission platform Selection Process The selection of the Vigdís Prize winner is carried out by an independent Selection Panel composed of experts in women’s empowerment. Composition of the Panel: Six independent members Three nominated by the Parliamentary Assembly Three nominated by the Government of Iceland Evaluation Criteria: Demonstrated impact on women’s empowerment Innovation and sustainability of the initiative Contribution to gender equality and inclusion Measurable outcomes and long-term potential Award Ceremony The Vigdís Prize 2026 will be awarded during an official ceremony held in Strasbourg, France, on 22 June 2026, as part of the Summer Session of the Parliamentary Assembly. The winner will be announced by the President of the Parliamentary Assembly, providing global recognition and visibility. Previous Winners The Vigdís Prize has already recognized impactful initiatives across the world: 2025 Winner: Women of the Sun (Palestine) – recognized for their work in women’s rights and peacebuilding 2024 Winner: Irida Women’s Center (Greece) – supporting women facing poverty, social exclusion, and gender-based violence These examples highlight the diversity and impact of initiatives honored by the prize. Why You Should Apply or Nominate The Vigdís Prize offers a unique opportunity to: Gain international recognition for impactful work Secure financial support to expand initiatives Join a global network of changemakers Amplify efforts in advancing gender equality Inspire others through proven impact How to Apply To apply or nominate a candidate: Carefully review the Official regulations Complete the Nomination form Gather at least five Sponsor signatures Compile all documents into one PDF Submit via the Official Platform before the deadline Deadline All nominations must be submitted by 27 March 2026. Late submissions will not be considered. Learn more about the Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026 HERE. Click HERE for more global Award opportunities on Opportunities for Youth. title: Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders in Africa, Asia and the Middle East to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town
summary: The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 is one of the most prestigious fully funded global leadership opportunities designed for young changemakers who are actively transforming their communities. Offered by One Young World , this scholarship provides selected candidates with a fully funded chance to attend the globally renowned One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town . About the Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship was created to empower emerging leaders who are addressing some of the most pressing global challenges affecting women, girls, and vulnerable populations. The initiative is named after Hauwa Ojeifo , a globally recognized advocate for mental health and gender equality. The scholarship aims to build a new generation of leaders equipped with the tools, networks, and global exposure needed to drive meaningful change in their communities. It focuses particularly on social impact areas such as mental health, gender-based violence, and reproductive health rights. What is the One Young World Summit? The One Young World Summit is a prestigious global event that gathers outstanding young leaders from across the world. Delegates participate in high-level discussions, networking sessions, and leadership training alongside global influencers, policymakers, and industry leaders. Participants also gain lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador community, enabling long-term collaboration and impact. Fully Funded Benefits of the Scholarship The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 provides a comprehensive funding package that removes financial barriers and ensures full participation: Full access to the One Young World Summit 2026 Round-trip airfare to Cape Town Hotel accommodation for the entire duration of the summit Daily meals (breakfast, lunch, and dinner) Ground transportation between accommodation and event venue Lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador network Access to leadership programs and global initiatives Opportunity to apply for funding of up to $25,000 for social impact projects Fields and Focus Areas Applicants must be actively working in at least one of the following impact areas: Mental health and psychosocial support Gender-based violence and domestic abuse prevention Sexual and reproductive health rights Maternal and child health Disability rights and inclusion Advocacy for the girl child (e.g., child marriage, early pregnancy) Non-communicable diseases These focus areas align with global development priorities and aim to support initiatives that create sustainable social impact. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of Applicants) This scholarship is designed for: Young leaders and changemakers Social entrepreneurs and nonprofit founders Community organizers and activists Professionals working in public health, gender equality, or human rights Individuals leading grassroots or national-level initiatives Eligibility Criteria (Detailed Requirements) To qualify for the Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026, applicants must meet the following criteria: 1. Age Requirement Must be between 18 and 30 years old Candidates above 30 may be considered if they demonstrate exceptional impact 2. Leadership and Impact Must hold a leadership role or lead a project/initiative Demonstrated measurable impact in their community 3. Thematic Alignment Work must align with at least one of the scholarship’s focus areas 4. Geographic Eligibility Applicants must live and create impact in Africa, Asia, or the Middle East 5. Language Requirement Proficiency in English (spoken and written) 6. Commitment Must be available to attend the summit and actively participate in all sessions Why You Should Apply for This Scholarship This opportunity goes beyond financial support. It offers: Global exposure and recognition Access to influential leaders and mentors Opportunities for funding and scaling your project A powerful international network of changemakers Personal and professional development through leadership training The scholarship is particularly impactful for individuals who are already making a difference but lack the global platform to amplify their work. Apply here Deadline 27th March 2026 The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 stands out as a transformative opportunity for young leaders dedicated to advancing gender equality, mental health awareness, and social justice. By removing financial barriers and connecting changemakers to a global platform, One Young World continues to empower the next generation of leaders who are shaping the future. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders in Africa, Asia and the Middle East to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for Youth Leaders for Peace Fellowship (YL4P) 2026 in India — Build Skills for Community Impact
summary: Are you an international-minded young leader working to create change in your community? The Youth Leaders for Peace Fellowship (YL4P) – India Chapter offers a unique opportunity to strengthen your leadership, expand your impact, and connect with a national network of changemakers committed to social justice and peacebuilding. Organized by the White Dot Solidarity Network, this fellowship is designed for young people across India who are already engaged in social action and are looking to deepen their skills, knowledge, and long-term impact. Through a structured online programme, participants will gain practical tools to sustain and elevate their work in areas such as human rights, climate change, gender justice, and community development. About the Fellowship The YL4P is a one-month intensive training programme that combines interactive learning sessions with hands-on community engagement. Running from 1 May to 1 July 2026 , the fellowship is delivered entirely online via Zoom, making it accessible to participants across India. The programme includes five structured training sessions held weekly, each lasting two hours. These sessions are complemented by a month-long community engagement assignment, where fellows apply their learning to real-world challenges within their communities. A distinctive feature of the fellowship is its intergenerational learning approach , where each session is co-facilitated by both youth experts and senior practitioners. This allows participants to gain diverse perspectives, practical insights, and mentorship from experienced leaders. The fellowship is supported by the Youth Empowerment Fund of Global Youth Mobilisation, funded by the European Union and a consortium of global youth organizations. Why This Opportunity Matters Young leaders working in social impact often face challenges such as limited resources, lack of structured training, and minimal access to networks. The YL4P Fellowship directly addresses these gaps by providing both skills and support systems. By combining training, mentorship, funding support, and networking, the programme equips participants to sustain and scale their work more effectively. It also creates a platform for collaboration among youth leaders across different regions and sectors. For individuals committed to creating meaningful change, this fellowship offers a practical pathway to strengthen both leadership capacity and community impact. What Participants Will Gain One of the strongest aspects of the YL4P Fellowship is its focus on practical, real-world impact rather than purely theoretical learning. Participants will gain structured training in key areas such as leadership, advocacy, research, fundraising, and digital security. These skills are essential for individuals working in social impact spaces and help strengthen both personal and organizational effectiveness. A major benefit of the programme is the community engagement assignment , where fellows design and implement a project related to their area of work. This hands-on component allows participants to directly apply their learning while making a tangible difference in their communities. Fellows will also receive an honorarium to support the costs associated with their community projects, making it easier to implement meaningful initiatives. In addition, participants gain access to ongoing mentorship , which helps them refine their ideas, overcome challenges, and strengthen their long-term impact strategies. Upon successful completion, fellows receive a certificate and become part of the White Dot Solidarity Network’s pan-India community of youth leaders. This network provides opportunities for collaboration, visibility, and future engagement. Who Can Apply The YL4P fellowship is open to young leaders who meet the following criteria: Aged between 18 and 35 years Currently residing in India Have 1–3 years of experience in community work (including volunteering, internships, or civil society engagement) Actively involved in a social intervention, initiative, or community-based project Demonstrate leadership and commitment to community development Have a working knowledge of English Programme Structure and Modules The YL4P fellowship includes weekly sessions covering key areas such as: Youth leadership and ethical community engagement Research methods and documentation Human rights frameworks and advocacy Resource mobilization and fundraising Digital security and protection practices The programme concludes with a project presentation session, followed by the final submission of community project reports. Application Process and Deadline Interested applicants must complete the online application form and s ubmit all required information before the deadline. Application Deadline: 10 April 2026 Incomplete applications will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to review their submissions carefully before applying. Apply through the official application form . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Apply for CSAE Predoctoral Fellow at University of Oxford 2026 — Work on Global Development Research
summary: Are you looking to gain hands-on experience in development economics research at a world-leading institution? The CSAE Predoctoral Fellow position at the University of Oxford offers an exceptional opportunity to work closely with leading researchers while contributing to impactful studies on global development challenges. Hosted within the Department of Economics, this role places the selected candidate in Associate Professor Dennis Egger’s research group, focusing on macro-experimental development, including the impacts of cash transfers and social protection programs in countries such as Malawi, Rwanda, and Ethiopia. This position is ideal for candidates planning to pursue a PhD or research-focused career, offering direct exposure to high-level academic research and policy-relevant work. About the Programme The Centre for the Study of African Economies (CSAE) at the University of Oxford is one of the world’s leading research centres dedicated to understanding economic development, particularly in Africa. The CSAE community brings together researchers working on poverty, policy, and economic transformation. As a Predoctoral Fellow, you will join a dynamic research environment within the Department of Economics. You will contribute to ongoing research projects while gaining valuable insights into experimental and empirical methods used in development economics. Working under the supervision of Associate Professor Dennis Egger, the role focuses on research examining how social protection programs — such as cash transfers — affect economic outcomes and livelihoods in low-income settings. Why This Opportunity Matters Predoctoral fellowships are a critical stepping stone for individuals aiming to pursue PhDs or careers in academic research. This position provides a rare opportunity to work within a globally recognized institution while contributing to meaningful, policy-relevant research. The experience gained through this role — from data analysis to research design — equips fellows with the skills needed to succeed in competitive graduate programs and research environments. Moreover, by focusing on real-world issues such as poverty and social protection, this fellowship allows participants to engage in work that has the potential to influence policy and improve lives. What Participants Will Gain One of the most significant advantages of this opportunity is the direct exposure to cutting-edge research in development economics . Selected fellows will work closely with academic researchers on active projects, contributing to data collection, analysis, and research outputs. This hands-on experience helps build strong technical and analytical skills that are essential for graduate-level study and research careers. Fellows will also gain experience using quantitative tools and methodologies commonly used in economics research, including data analysis, experimental design, and impact evaluation techniques. In addition, the position offers the opportunity to be part of the wider CSAE research network. This includes engaging with seminars, workshops, and discussions led by leading economists, providing exposure to diverse research topics and approaches. The role also significantly strengthens academic and professional profiles. Experience at the University of Oxford is highly regarded and can support future applications for PhD programs, research positions, and policy roles. Finally, working on projects focused on social protection and poverty reduction allows fellows to contribute to research that has real-world policy implications and societal impact. Who Can Apply This opportunity is ideal for individuals who are preparing for advanced study or a career in economics or related fields. Applicants typically have: A strong academic background in economics, development studies, or related disciplines Quantitative and analytical skills An interest in development economics and policy research Experience or familiarity with data analysis tools (such as Stata, R, or Python) Candidates who are motivated, detail-oriented, and interested in research related to poverty, social protection, and economic development are particularly well-suited for this role. Research Focus Areas The fellowship will involve research on macro-experimental development, with a particular focus on: Cash transfer programs Social protection systems Economic impacts in low-income countries Field research in Malawi, Rwanda, and Ethiopia These areas are highly relevant to global development policy and contribute to understanding how interventions can improve livelihoods and reduce poverty. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must apply through the official University of Oxford recruitment system and submit all required documents, including academic transcripts and supporting materials. Application Deadline: 06 April 2026 Early preparation is recommended, as the selection process is competitive and requires strong academic and research credentials. Learn more and apply through the official Oxford CSAE page . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Apply for Fulbright-Nehru Doctoral Research Fellowships 2027–2028
summary: Are you an Indian PhD scholar looking to advance your research on a global stage? The Fulbright-Nehru Doctoral Research Fellowships offer a prestigious, fully funded opportunity to conduct research in the United States while building international academic networks and expertise. Administered by the United States-India Educational Foundation , this fellowship supports Indian doctoral students in carrying out research essential to their thesis at leading U.S. institutions. The program is designed to strengthen academic collaboration between India and the United States while addressing global challenges through innovative research. About the Fellowship The Fulbright-Nehru Doctoral Research Fellowship is a pre-doctoral program aimed at Indian scholars currently enrolled in PhD programs. It allows selected candidates to spend 6 to 9 months in the United States conducting research that contributes directly to their dissertation. The fellowship emphasizes research areas that are innovative, future-focused, and aligned with priority global challenges. Scholars are placed at U.S. universities or research institutions where they can access advanced facilities, academic mentorship, and interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities. Why This Opportunity Matters The Fulbright-Nehru Doctoral Research Fellowship is one of the most prestigious international research opportunities available to Indian scholars. It provides not only financial support but also access to global research ecosystems that can significantly enhance the quality and impact of doctoral work. By enabling scholars to work with leading institutions in the United States, the program helps bridge knowledge gaps, foster innovation, and promote cross-border academic collaboration. For many researchers, this fellowship can be a turning point — strengthening their academic profile, expanding career opportunities, and positioning them for future leadership roles in academia, policy, or industry. What Participants Will Gain One of the most significant advantages of the Fulbright-Nehru fellowship is its comprehensive financial and academic support . Selected fellows receive: A monthly stipend to cover living expenses Round-trip international airfare J-1 visa support Health and accident coverage A settling-in allowance and professional support funds Beyond funding, the fellowship offers access to world-class academic environments. Fellows can audit non-degree courses, collaborate with leading researchers, and gain exposure to advanced methodologies and technologies. Participants also benefit from international networking opportunities , connecting with scholars, institutions, and professionals that can support long-term research collaborations and career growth. The experience not only strengthens academic work but also enhances global perspectives, cultural understanding, and leadership skills. Who Can Apply The fellowship is open to: Indian citizens currently registered for a PhD at an Indian institution Scholars whose research contributes directly to their doctoral thesis Applicants with strong academic backgrounds and demonstrated research potential Applicants must have completed sufficient groundwork in their research area and clearly justify the need to conduct research in the United States. Important eligibility considerations include: PhD registration must be completed by the specified date (as outlined in the official call) The thesis submission date must be at least three months after the fellowship ends A recommendation letter from the PhD supervisor is required Government employees in certain services are not eligible Fields of Study The fellowship supports research across a range of priority areas, particularly those addressing global and technological challenges. These may include: Artificial Intelligence and advanced computing Bioengineering and materials science Critical minerals and supply chains Interdisciplinary scientific collaboration Applicants are encouraged to propose innovative and impactful research aligned with these areas. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must submit their applications through the official Fulbright-Nehru online portal, including all required documents such as research proposals, academic transcripts, and recommendation letters. Application Deadline: 1 July 2026 (23:59 IST) The selection process includes application review, interviews, and final approval by the Fulbright Foreign Scholarship Board. Successful applicants typically begin their program in August or September 2027 . Learn more and apply through the official USIEF website. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Apply for IIT Bhubaneswar Summer Internship 2026 — Gain Hands-On Research Experience in India
summary: Are you a student looking to gain real-world research experience at a leading institution? The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) of Bhubaneswar is now accepting applications for its Summer Internship Programme , offering students the opportunity to work closely with faculty members and explore advanced research across multiple disciplines. This internship is designed for students from various academic backgrounds who want to deepen their subject knowledge, build technical skills, and gain exposure to research environments at one of India’s premier institutes. About the Internship Programme The IIT Bhubaneswar Summer Internship Programme provides students with the opportunity to engage in short-term research projects under the supervision of faculty members. The programme spans multiple academic schools, including: Basic Sciences Earth, Ocean Climate Sciences Electrical and Computer Sciences Infrastructure and Mechanical Sciences Humanities, Social Sciences, and Management Minerals, Metallurgical, and Materials Engineering The internship typically takes place during the summer vacation period and allows participants to gain hands-on experience in academic research and project-based learning. Why This Opportunity Matters Participating in an internship at IIT Bhubaneswar can be a valuable stepping stone for students aiming to build careers in research, academia, or technical industries. The programme provides early exposure to research environments, helping students understand how academic knowledge is applied to real-world problems. This experience is particularly beneficial for those planning to pursue postgraduate studies or research careers. Additionally, working under experienced faculty members allows interns to receive mentorship, feedback, and guidance that can shape their academic and professional paths. The opportunity to engage with peers from different institutions and disciplines also fosters collaboration and broadens perspectives. What Participants Will Gain One of the key strengths of this internship is the opportunity to experience research in a structured academic environment. Participants will work directly with faculty mentors, allowing them to gain insights into research methodologies, problem-solving approaches, and advanced academic concepts. This exposure is particularly valuable for students considering higher studies such as a Master’s or PhD. The internship also provides access to institutional resources, including laboratories, libraries, and digital tools. Interns may receive temporary access to campus facilities, enabling them to fully immerse themselves in the research environment. In addition to academic learning, students develop important transferable skills such as critical thinking, data analysis, technical communication, and collaboration. Another potential benefit is financial support. While not guaranteed, some faculty supervisors may offer fellowships or stipends , depending on available project funding. Overall, the programme helps students build a stronger academic profile and gain experience that can support future applications for higher education or research positions. Who Can Apply The internship is open to: Students currently enrolled in a degree programme at any academic institution Applicants from diverse disciplines aligned with IIT Bhubaneswar’s academic schools Individuals interested in research, innovation, and academic development Internship Duration and Structure The Summer Internship Programme runs for 3 to 8 weeks during the institute’s summer vacation period. Interns work under the guidance of faculty supervisors and participate in research activities, which may include: Literature review and data collection Laboratory work or simulations Project development and analysis Report writing or presentations The experience is designed to provide both theoretical and practical exposure to research. Application Process and Deadline Applications must be submitted online through the official IIT Bhubaneswar portal . Offline or email applications are not accepted. Applicants should ensure all required details and documents are completed before submission, as late applications will not be considered. Application Deadline: 15 April 2026 Selected candidates will be notified via email, and the final list will be published on the institute’s website. Learn more and apply through the official IIT Bhubaneswar internship page . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for IIT Bhubaneswar Summer Internship 2026 — Gain Hands-On Research Experience in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ASEAN–Japan Young Environmental Leaders Network (AJYELN) 2.0 2026: Fully Funded Youth Programme for Plastic Action, Innovation & Regional Impact
summary: The ASEAN–Japan Young Environmental Leaders Network (AJYELN) 2.0 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exciting and impactful opportunity for young changemakers across ASEAN countries and Japan to lead innovative solutions addressing plastic pollution. With the theme “Youth for a Plastic-Resilient Region: Action. Innovation. Transformation.” , this programme goes beyond awareness by empowering youth to design, implement, and scale real-world environmental projects . It combines funding, training, mentorship, and regional collaboration to nurture the next generation of environmental leaders. This initiative is organized by the ASEAN-Japan Centre and aims to build a network of young leaders committed to tackling the growing plastic pollution crisis in the region. About the AJYELN 2.0 Programme The ASEAN–Japan Young Environmental Leaders Network (AJYELN) is a regional platform that brings together youth leaders from ASEAN Member States and Japan to develop and implement community-driven environmental solutions . The programme focuses specifically on plastic pollution and sustainability , encouraging participants to move from ideas to action by launching projects that create measurable impact. Each year, 11 teams (one per ASEAN country and Japan) are selected to become fellows and receive funding and support to implement their initiatives. Programme Theme for 2026 The 2026 edition focuses on: “Youth for a Plastic-Resilient Region: Action. Innovation. Transformation.” Applicants are expected to design projects that contribute to: Marine plastic waste reduction Plastic pollution management Waste and wastewater systems Circular economy solutions for plastics Types of Projects Encouraged Applicants should propose initiatives that incorporate: Community Engagement Mobilizing local communities Driving behavioral change Awareness Initiatives Campaigns and educational outreach Promoting sustainable practices Capacity Building and Collaboration Networking and partnerships Strengthening local environmental action Projects must be practical, community-based, and impact-driven , rather than purely research-focused. Benefits of Joining AJYELN Selected fellows will gain access to a comprehensive support system that includes: 1. Seed Funding Up to 100,000 JPY per team to implement environmental projects 2. Training and Capacity Building Leadership training Project management training Online self-paced learning modules 3. Mentorship and Guidance Continuous support throughout project implementation Expert feedback and advisory 4. Regional Networking Join a network of youth leaders across ASEAN and Japan Collaborate with peers and professionals 5. International Exposure Participate in in-person training in Jakarta, Indonesia Attend the AJYELN Symposium (2027) Key Dates and Timeline Applicants must take note of the following timeline: Application Phase Application Opens: 6 March 2026 Application Deadline: 15 April 2026 Selection Process Shortlisted Announcement: 15 May 2026 Interviews (Pitching): 22 May 2026 Final Selection Announcement: 5 June 2026 Programme Timeline Online Training: June – July 2026 In-Person Training (Jakarta): 11–13 August 2026 Seed Funding Disbursement: 30 August 2026 Project Implementation: September – December 2026 Final Reports Deadline: 31 January 2027 Capstone Symposium: March 2027 Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: A team of 2–3 members Aged 18–25 years old Citizens of ASEAN Member States or Japan Proficient in conversational English Passionate about environmental and social impact Applicants do not need to be university students—youth from all backgrounds are encouraged to apply. Application Requirements To apply, applicants must submit: Completed application form Passport or citizenship ID (for each team member) Team profile and background Project proposal (using official template) 1-minute pitch video (uploaded online with a shareable link) Applications must be submitted via: https://bit.ly/join_AJYELN2026 Ensure all required documents are submitted before the deadline. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Motivation and experience (20%) Project feasibility (30%) Impact potential (30%) Creativity and innovation (20%) Programme Commitment Selected fellows must: Participate in all training sessions Implement their project within the timeline Submit financial and impact reports Attend the final symposium Failure to comply may require reimbursement of funding and program costs. Why You Should Apply The AJYELN 2.0 programme offers a unique opportunity to: Turn your ideas into real environmental impact Gain hands-on leadership and project experience Access funding and mentorship Build a regional and international network Contribute to solving one of the region’s biggest challenges—plastic pollution This is more than a programme—it is a platform to lead change, innovate solutions, and inspire communities . Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. What is AJYELN? AJYELN is a regional network of young environmental leaders working to combat plastic pollution across ASEAN and Japan. 2. Can I apply as an individual? No. Applicants must apply as a team of 2–3 members . 3. Do I need to be a university student? No. Youth from all backgrounds are eligible as long as they meet the age and other requirements. 4. Can the funding be used for research? No. Projects must focus on community engagement, awareness, and capacity building , not research-only activities. 5. Is the programme fully funded? Yes. The programme provides seed funding, training, and support , but participants must ensure availability for required activities. 6. Can team members be changed after selection? No. Teams must remain unchanged after acceptance. 7. What happens if I withdraw? Participants may withdraw, but may need to repay funding and associated costs . 8. What language is used? The programme is conducted in English . Learn more about AJYELN 2026 HERE. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE. The post ASEAN–Japan Young Environmental Leaders Network (AJYELN) 2.0 2026: Fully Funded Youth Programme for Plastic Action, Innovation Regional Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Marin County Summer 2026 Internships (Undergraduate & Graduate): Paid Public Policy Opportunities in California
summary: The County of Marin is offering two highly competitive paid internships for Summer 2026 through the Office of the County Executive and the Board of Supervisors. These opportunities are designed for students interested in public service, governance, sustainability, and policy-making. Located in San Rafael, California , this program provides hands-on experience in real government operations while allowing interns to contribute directly to community-focused initiatives. 1. Undergraduate Internship – Office of the County Executive / Board of Supervisors Overview The Undergraduate Internship is a part-time, paid opportunity designed for students who want to gain early exposure to public policy and local government operations. Interns will work within a cohort-based structure , meaning they will collaborate with other interns while also contributing individually to specific divisions. This model encourages both teamwork and professional development. Key Details Start Date: June 22, 2026 Duration: Late summer (August/September depending on academic schedule) Hours: 15–20 hours per week Schedule: Monday–Thursday (10:00 AM – 3:30 PM) Location: On-site in San Rafael, California Salary $20.10 – $24.42 per hour What You Will Do Undergraduate interns will support various functions within the County Executive’s Office or Board of Supervisors, including: Assisting with policy and budget research Supporting community engagement initiatives Contributing to communications and outreach Participating in climate and sustainability projects Observing and assisting in public meetings and governance processes Learning Experience This internship is designed to go beyond administrative tasks. Interns will: Attend learning sessions and workshops Participate in site visits across Marin County departments Join coordinated trips to Sacramento and San Francisco Gain exposure to regional and state-level governance Internship Placements Interns may be placed in divisions such as: Budget Legislative Affairs Climate Sustainability Office of Equity Communications Digital Transformation Municipal Services Board of Supervisors Offices Eligibility (Undergraduate) Applicants must: Be currently enrolled in a university OR Be within one year of graduation OR Be in a supervised licensure-related program For more information click here APPLY HERE 2. Graduate Internship – Office of the County Executive / Board of Supervisors Overview The Graduate Internship is designed for students pursuing advanced degrees who want deeper involvement in policy development, strategic planning, and public administration. While the structure is similar to the undergraduate program, graduate interns are expected to take on more analytical, research-based, and strategic responsibilities . Key Details Start Date: June 22, 2026 Duration: Late summer (August/September) Hours: 15–20 hours per week Schedule: Monday–Thursday (10:00 AM – 3:30 PM) Location: On-site in San Rafael, California Salary $23.12 – $28.09 per hour What You Will Do Graduate interns will engage in more advanced tasks, such as: Conducting policy analysis and research Supporting legislative tracking and advocacy efforts Contributing to budget planning and financial strategy Assisting with climate resilience and sustainability initiatives Supporting equity-focused policy development Advanced Learning Opportunities Graduate interns benefit from: Exposure to high-level decision-making processes Direct involvement in strategic government initiatives Opportunities to collaborate with policy experts and leaders Participation in the same cohort-based learning and field visits Internship Placements Graduate interns have access to the same divisions as undergraduate interns, but with greater responsibility and analytical depth , including: Budget Finance Legislative Affairs Climate Policy Equity Inclusion Communications Digital Innovation Municipal Services Board of Supervisors Offices Eligibility (Graduate) Applicants must: Be enrolled in a graduate program , OR Be within one year of graduation, OR Be participating in a supervised licensure program Deadline April 5, 2026 (11:59 PM Pacific Time) For more information click here APPLY HERE Why You Should Apply Both internships provide an exceptional opportunity to: Gain real-world experience in government Build a strong professional network Develop leadership and policy skills Contribute to impactful community initiatives Whether you are an undergraduate exploring career paths or a graduate student refining your expertise, this program offers a strong foundation for careers in public policy, governance, international relations, and development . Discover more opportunities here The post Marin County Summer 2026 Internships (Undergraduate Graduate): Paid Public Policy Opportunities in California appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
